channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-11
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `statcheck` and `escicheck` projects have made updates to their respective branches, with `statcheck` merging the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch and `escicheck` synchronizing changes from the `escicheckapp` repository. Meanwhile, `docpluck` has addressed issues with residual prose shapes and affiliation remnants, and `coarse` has re-enabled Fable 5 in the model picker. Additionally, `auto-zcurve` has revised its validation note to include a citation, and `output-it-forward` has added a convenient Chrome Webstore link for easier access. These updates demonstrate the collaborative spirit and commitment to improvement that defines our community, and we look forward to seeing the continued impact of these projects.

29 commits across 3 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

metacheck16 commits
regcheck10 commits
docpluck3 commits

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed, specifically merging the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch from the repository `https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck` into itself. The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch also received "admin updates", as stated in the commit message. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commit messages. The merge commit and the admin updates commit are the only two changes made since the last update. The nature of the "admin updates" is not specified beyond the commit message. Only the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was affected by these changes.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 4 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The first phase of development involved adding "registered report mode", as indicated by the commit message "first phase: registered report mode". The second phase of development, or "stage 2 RR dev", was then implemented, building on the initial work. A third phase of RR creation was also added, as described in the commit message "third phase of RR creation". However, some of this work was later reverted, with a commit to "roll back RR overkill", suggesting that some changes were deemed excessive.

4 commit(s) today:

  • cfeac5d [staging] roll back RR overkill — Jamie Cummins
  • 35075aa [staging] third phase of RR creation — Jamie Cummins
  • 6d30479 [staging] stage 2 RR dev — Jamie Cummins
  • 60f395c [staging] first phase: registered report mode — Jamie Cummins
  • 2363d45 [staging] small fix — Jamie Cummins
  • 8277b4d [staging] budget-saving changes — Jamie Cummins
  • f8a0f65 [staging] improve specificity on 'missing' judgements — Jamie Cummins
  • f02456c [staging] update default openai model — Jamie Cummins
  • 90c62a8 [staging] aesthetic bug fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • efb74a8 [staging] improve backend handling of insufficient evidence — Jamie Cummins
  • 1f6986b [staging] remove sign-up — Jamie Cummins
  • 717d756 [staging] user survey improvment — Jamie Cummins
  • 8c5b3bf [staging] additional aesthetic imprvoements — Jamie Cummins
  • adef1de [staging] minor aesthetic improvements — Jamie Cummins
  • a3141f9 [staging] fix quote tracing — Jamie Cummins
  • a8a5cbe [staging] improve report retention — Jamie Cummins
  • d6c7e96 [staging] add multi-file upload — Jamie Cummins
  • 72f7d60 [staging] add advanced error handling and regeneration — Jamie Cummins
  • f0792b4 [staging] add html export for report; various QOL improvements — Jamie Cummins
  • ae1de84 [staging] hardening multi-study extractions — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →
